topics: 🚫 Konam vows: 'בחיי ובמותי' — does the prohibition extend past death?
🏠 The 'בית זה' / 'נכסים אילו' rule: demonstrative words bind beyond life
👤 Robbing a ger who dies — keren and chomesh to kohanim, asham to the altar
📖 R' Yose HaGalili vs. R' Akiva: who is the verse 'אם אין לאיש גואל' speaking about?
⛪ Mishmar division: money to Yehoiarib, asham to Yedaiah

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Konam Vows and the Stolen Ger
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: The daf opens with a father's konam against his son's enjoyment, and asks how 'בחיי ובמותי' is different from saying each phrase alone. R' Yose answers with a parallel from Nedarim: a demonstrative word like 'בית זה' or 'נכסים אילו' binds the property both during life and after death. The sugya then turns to one who robbed a ger and swore falsely: when the ger dies with no relatives, the keren and chomesh go to the kohanim and the asham to the altar. The Amoraim debate whose Mishnah this follows — R' Yose HaGalili's reading or R' Akiva's.
overview.hebrewSource: הָאוֹמֵר לִבְנוֹ קוֹנָם שֶׁאַתָּה נֶהֱנֶה לִי אִם מֵת יוֹרִישֶׁנּוּ. בְּחַיָּי וּבְמוֹתִי אִם מֵת לֹא יוֹרִישֶׁנּוּ

slides.title: 🚫 Three Konam Phrasings — Three Outcomes
slides.subtitle: R' Jeremiah's puzzle: why does 'בחיי ובמותי' alone block inheritance?

boxes.title: 🟢 בחיי alone
items: Konam binds only during the father's life

boxes.title: 🟡 במותי alone
items: Phrasing bound to time of death
Inheritance is not 'הנאה' from the father

boxes.title: 🔴 בחיי ובמותי
items: Property goes to brothers/sons or to creditors

callout.label: R' Jeremiah's question:
callout.text: מַה בֵין אָהֵן דָּמַר חָדָא חָדָא לָהֵן דָּמַר תַּרְתֵּי תַּרְתֵּי — what is the difference between saying it one-by-one and saying it as a pair?

slides.title: 💬 R' Yose: Nedarim vs. Nezikin
slides.subtitle: The demonstrative word 'this' / 'these' is what binds beyond life.

lines.speaker: RJ
lines.text: Rav Jeremiah and R' Yose ben Ḥanina: קוֹנָם לְבַיִת זֶה שֶׁאֵינִי נִכְנַס בְחַיַּי וּבְמוֹתִי — once he said 'this house', he forbade it both in life and after death.

lines.speaker: RY
lines.text: R' Yose: תַּנִּינָן בִּנְזָקִין מַה דְלֹא תַנִּינָן בִּנְדָרִין — we taught in Neziqin what we didn't teach in Nedarim.

lines.speaker: RY
lines.text: קוֹנָם הֲנָיַית נְכָסַיי אֵילּוּ עָלַי בְּחַיַּי וּבְמוֹתִי — once he says 'these' my properties, he forbids them on himself in life and after death.

lines.speaker: Y
lines.text: The trigger word is the demonstrative — זה / אילו — which fixes the prohibition onto the object itself rather than onto the time period.

callout.label: Yerushalmi insight:
callout.text: Nezikin makes explicit a halakhah that Nedarim left implicit: words of demonstration bind property permanently.

slides.title: 👤 The Stolen Ger Who Died
slides.subtitle: Mishnah 9:9 — what happens to the gezeilah when the ger has no heirs?

nodes.content:
הַגּוֹזֵל אֶת הַגֵּר וְנִשְׁבַּע לוֹ וָמֵת
One robbed a ger, swore falsely, the ger died
Verse: וְאִם אֵין לָאִישׁ גּוֹאֵל לְהָשִׁיב הָאָשָׁם אֵלָיו

columns.label: If money already given
columns.content:
→ heirs cannot reclaim
אִישׁ אֲשֶׁר יִתֵּן לַכֹּהֶן לוֹ יִהְיֶה

nodes.content: If the robber dies en route, the asham grazes until blemished, is sold, and proceeds become nedavah

callout.label: Why this works only for a ger:
callout.text: Only a convert can lack relatives entirely. A born Jew always has some 'גואל' — a tribe relative who would inherit.

slides.title: 📜 R' Yose HaGalili vs. R' Akiva
slides.subtitle: Two Tannaitic readings of 'אם אין לאיש גואל'.
rows: הַטָּעוּן כַּפָּרָה חַיָיב. יָצָא זֶה שֶׁאֵין טָעוּן כַּפָּרָה
Verse applies only when atonement is needed — i.e. theft + false oath
רִבִּי עֲקִיבָה (when he came from Zephyrium)
אֵין לָךְ אָדָם בְּיִשְׂרָאֵל שֶׁאֵין לוֹ גוֹאֵל אֶלָּא זֶה הַגֵּר
Only a ger fits 'אין גואל' — born Jews always have a relative
Rav + Reish Lakish on the Mishnah
Aligns with R' Akiva: stolen property must be returned in any case

callout.label: The dispute:
callout.text: Ḥilfai and R' Yose bar Ḥanina trace the Mishnah to R' Yose HaGalili; Rav and Reish Lakish trace it to R' Akiva.

slides.title: ⛪ Mishnah 9:10 — Mishmar Division of the Asham
slides.subtitle: Who gets the money, who gets the offering?

steps.content: 1️⃣ Money to Yehoiarib + asham to Yedaiah → he has fulfilled his obligation.
2️⃣ Asham to Yehoiarib + money to Yedaiah — if the offering is still alive, Yedaiah's people offer it.
3️⃣ Otherwise: he must bring another asham.
4️⃣ Rule: הַמֵּבִיא גְזֵילוֹ עַד שֶׁלֹּא הֵבִיא אֲשָׁמוֹ יָצָא — bringing the stolen item before the asham works; the reverse does not.
5️⃣ נָתַן אֶת הַקֶּרֶן וְלֹא נָתַן אֶת הַחוֹמֶשׁ אֵין חוֹמֶשׁ מְעַכֵּב — the chomesh is not me'akev.

callout.label: Bottom line:
callout.text: The robber must restore the principal before atonement; the chomesh is a side-payment that doesn't block kapparah.

summary.principle: Demonstrative words ('this', 'these') bind a vow beyond life; and a ger's stolen property goes to the kohanim and altar — provided the principal is returned before the asham is offered.

flowSteps.content:
Opening puzzle
בְּחַיַּי וּבְמוֹתִי blocks inheritance — why?
R' Yose: the demonstrative word ('בית זה' / 'נכסים אילו') is what binds beyond life

🎓 Key Principle:
Restitution comes before atonement; the principal must reach the right hand before the offering reaches the altar.

quiz.q: Why does 'בחיי ובמותי' block the son from inheriting, while 'בחיי' or 'במותי' alone does not?
o: Because saying both phrases together creates a continuous prohibition spanning life and death
Because it doubles the konam's strength
Because death automatically dissolves all vows
Because the son becomes a creditor, not an heir
quiz.e: R' Jeremiah notes that combining 'בחיי' + 'במותי' uniquely binds both periods. R' Yose explains the mechanism: only with a demonstrative anchor (this/these) does the prohibition extend post-mortem.

quiz.q: According to R' Yose's Nezikin teaching, what triggers a konam to bind property after death?
o: Saying 'אילו' / 'זה' — a demonstrative word fixing the vow on the object itself
Saying the vow at sunset
Naming three witnesses
Repeating the vow three times
quiz.e: קוֹנָם לְבַיִת זֶה / נְכָסַיי אֵילּוּ — the demonstrative anchors the prohibition to the object permanently.

quiz.q: When a robber stole from a ger who then died with no relatives, what does the verse 'אם אין לאיש גואל' require?
o: Keren and chomesh to the kohanim, asham to the altar
Everything to the king's treasury
Everything to the local court
He keeps it as ownerless
quiz.e: The Mishnah: מְשַׁלֵּם קֶרֶן וָחוֹמֶשׁ לַכֹּהֲנִים וְאָשָׁם לַמִּזְבֵּחַ.

quiz.q: What is R' Akiva's reading of 'אם אין לאיש גואל'?
o: Only a ger has no relative — every born Jew has a גואל somewhere
Only a kohen has no relative
It refers to one who needs atonement
It refers to a deathbed gift
quiz.e: אֵין לָךְ אָדָם בְּיִשְׂרָאֵל שֶׁאֵין לוֹ גוֹאֵל אֶלָּא זֶה הַגֵּר — only the convert truly has no relative.

quiz.q: What is the rule about bringing the stolen item versus the asham?
o: Bringing the gezeilah before the asham works; bringing the asham first does not
Either order is fine
Asham must always come first
Both must be brought simultaneously
quiz.e: הַמֵּבִיא גְזֵילוֹ עַד שֶׁלֹּא הֵבִיא אֲשָׁמוֹ יָצָא. אֲשָׁמוֹ עַד שֶׁלֹּא הֵבִיא גְזֵילוֹ לֹא יָצָא. Restitution must precede atonement.
